questions.prompt: Which BEST distinguishes project closure from project completion?
choices: Completion occurs after all debriefs are submitted
Reaching the final deliverable automatically means the project is closed
A project is only fully complete when formal closure is executed
Closure only applies to failed projects

questions.prompt: A project is stopped because the organisation's strategic priorities shifted. This is:
choices: Normal closure
Premature closure
Changed priority closure
Failed project

questions.prompt: Which statement BEST explains why retrospectives are more disciplined than lessons learned?
choices: Retrospectives are only conducted at project end
Retrospectives are shorter and less structured
Retrospectives are conducted at regular intervals, are structured, and produce concrete improvement actions
Retrospectives replace the risk register in Agile

questions.prompt: In Scrum, the Product Owner's PRIMARY responsibility is:
choices: Facilitating ceremonies and removing blockers
Acting as the business representative, setting feature priority, and accepting completed work
Managing day-to-day development tasks
Overseeing the entire programme portfolio

questions.prompt: Which of the following is NOT one of the six wrap-up closure activities?
choices: Obtain formal customer acceptance
Close vendor contracts
Produce a final project report
Begin the next project without releasing resources

questions.prompt: In Agile, a Sprint is best described as:
choices: A final review meeting at project end
A short time-boxed cycle (1-4 weeks) delivering working output
A tool used to estimate project costs
A document defining all requirements before development

questions.prompt: A decision to terminate a failed project should be based on:
choices: The amount of money already invested (sunk cost)
The future value the project can still deliver
The number of team members currently assigned
The original project charter objectives

questions.prompt: The Scrum Master's role is to:
choices: Define product features and priorities
Write code and deliver work increments
Facilitate the Scrum process and remove blockers
Manage the project budget and timeline

questions.prompt: How does Agile differ from traditional (waterfall) project management?
choices: Agile has longer phases and more documentation
Agile is iterative and adaptive; waterfall is sequential and plan-heavy with fixed scope
Agile uses a WBS and network diagram; waterfall does not
Agile requires a larger team

questions.prompt: Which is a characteristic of PREMATURE closure?
choices: The project is terminated because all objectives were met early
The project is stopped before objectives are fully met
The project is paused due to a budget freeze
The project is extended because of scope changes

questions.prompt: A retrospective repository that classifies past projects by type, size, and technology is valuable because:
choices: It reduces the need for a project charter
It enables future teams to consult past lessons and avoid repeat mistakes
It replaces the risk register
It speeds up approval processes

questions.prompt: In a Scrum Sprint, the Daily Scrum is used to:
choices: Review the entire product backlog with the Product Owner
Formally accept completed deliverables
Briefly align the team on daily progress, plans, and blockers (15 minutes max)
Plan the next sprint and assign all new tasks

questions.prompt: Which wrap-up closure activity ensures the organisation is no longer legally obligated to suppliers?
choices: Produce final project report
Release project team members
Close vendor contracts and supplier obligations
Obtain formal customer acceptance

questions.prompt: Which statement about the Product Backlog in Scrum is CORRECT?
choices: It is fixed and cannot change once the project starts
It is maintained and reprioritised continuously by the Product Owner
It is written by the Scrum Master at project initiation
It replaces the WBS in Agile projects

questions.prompt: The six wrap-up activities in project closure are significant because they:
choices: Accelerate the start of the next project
Formally discharge all project obligations across people, contracts, systems, and knowledge
Replace the need for a retrospective
Ensure the budget is fully spent
